assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 14 states 'Ecosystem | RPM'. Line 32 explains: 'The vulnerable package openssl-libs is a system RPM package. The 2.2.x stream’s Ecosystem Mappings table lists RPM with lock file rpms.lock.yaml and check command git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml.' This matches the SKILL.md ecosystem detection step which requires reading ecosystems from the Ecosystem Mappings config table. The detection correctly identifies RPM from the library name openssl-libs and references the config table.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 9-16 include all five versions: 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, and 2.2.4 in the version impact table. Line 7 states 'Dependency versions extracted from rpms.lock.yaml at each pinned tag in the supportability matrix.' The table header includes 'openssl-libs (rpms.lock.yaml)' confirming rpms.lock.yaml as the data source. Each version row shows the extracted openssl-libs version from the lock file (e.g., 3.0.7-25.el9_3 for 2.2.0, 3.0.7-27.el9_4 for 2.2.1, etc.).

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 26-28 state: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. Line 35 further confirms: 'The package openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml at each inspected tag, confirming it is an explicitly installed RPM (not inherited from the base image).' The lock file presence is used as the primary classification signal, with SBOM verification noted as supplementary (and skipped).

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 5 states: 'RPM ecosystem produces a single remediation task targeting the Konflux release repo. No upstream backport task is needed because system packages are updated directly in the Konflux release repo lock files.' Only one jira.create_issue call is shown (lines 10-16). The task targets the Konflux release repo 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 23). data-extraction.md line 32 also confirms: 'remediation produces a single task (Konflux repo fix), not the two-task upstream+downstream pattern used for source dependency ecosystems.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains: '## Repository' (line 22, 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z'), '## Target Branch' (line 26, 'main'), '## Description' (line 28), '## Implementation Notes' (line 52), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 67). There is no '## Files to Modify' section present, which is correct per remediation-templates.md line 186: 'Omitted sections: Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.' The template also includes Test Requirements and Dependencies sections consistent with the system package template in remediation-templates.md.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 28 states: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' This matches the required skip notice format from version-impact-analysis.md lines 265-268 which specifies the warning: 'SBOM verification skipped — cosign not available / SBOM download failed. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' Line 35 further explains: 'SBOM cross-verification via cosign was not performed because cosign is not available in this environment. The rpms.lock.yaml classification is used as the sole source of truth.' This satisfies the mandatory output requirement from version-impact-analysis.md lines 270-274.
